text.title: EV for Fixed-Bid Outsource Core Rules
text.summary: The two-ledger discipline that lets a fixed-bid PM track margin without losing the client-facing scope-completion view.

rule.statement: Run two parallel ledgers per engagement: ledger-A "client scope" records PV-S (planned scope value), EV-S (scoped value earned), no AC because the client price is fixed; ledger-B "vendor margin" records PV-C (planned internal cost), EV-C (cost-budget earned proportional to EV-S), AC-C (actual internal labour cost). SPI is computed once (EV-S/PV-S); CPI is computed only on ledger B (EV-C/AC-C).
rule.rationale: Mixing scope and margin into one ledger hides the failure mode the variant exists to catch: scope can be 100% complete on time while margin is destroyed. Two ledgers separate "did we deliver" from "did we earn".

rule.statement: Work packages are sized at 1-5% of the total scope value each. Larger packages hide partial-completion noise; smaller packages create overhead without earning fidelity. Engagements with too-large WBS rebalance before ledger-B rollout.
rule.rationale: Earned-value at sub-percent granularity becomes accounting; at ≥10% it becomes binary (done/not-done). The 1-5% band is where the curves remain informative without drowning the PM in updates.

rule.statement: Change requests are recorded as ledger-A scope deltas at the client-approved price (PV-S adjusts, the locked total adjusts) AND ledger-B cost-budget deltas at the internally-estimated cost (PV-C adjusts). The two deltas must be approved together; one-side-only updates are forbidden and rejected by the tracker.
rule.rationale: Single-side change updates are the most common cause of mid-engagement margin surprise — scope grows on the client side, cost-budget never grows on the vendor side, AC eventually catches up and the margin disappears silently.

rule.statement: Three early-warning metrics are computed weekly and trigger documented actions: (1) CPI-C < 0.85 → margin-recovery plan within 7 days; (2) SPI-S < 0.85 → scope or schedule re-plan within 7 days; (3) cumulative AC-C > 60% with EV-C < 50% at midpoint → executive escalation. Each metric has a named owner.
rule.rationale: Without thresholds and named owners the metrics decay into a dashboard nobody acts on. Documented thresholds plus owners convert numbers into routed work.

rule.statement: At engagement close the two ledgers reconcile in a single closure record: ledger-A reports scope completion (% of contracted value delivered); ledger-B reports realised margin (contracted revenue − final AC-C); the variance between PV-C at signature and AC-C at close is decomposed by attributed cause (estimation error, scope creep accepted without CR, productivity, rework). Closure without reconciliation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without attributed variance the team cannot improve estimates on the next bid; the same margin hole reopens. Forced attribution at close is the only mechanism that feeds the next bid model.

example.code: Engagement: P4-2026-acme-migration (fixed bid $180k) Ledger A (client scope): PV-S=$180k, EV-S=$108k (60% complete), SPI=1.02 Ledger B (vendor margin): PV-C=$135k (budgeted internal cost), EV-C=$81k, AC-C=$93k → CPI-C=0.87 Action: CPI-C < 0.90 → margin-recovery plan: cut 0.5 FTE rework on module X, re-baseline WBS 7-9. CR-014 approved 2026-05-12: scope +$12k (PV-S → $192k), cost +$8k (PV-C → $143k), both sides recorded same day.
SPI: 1.02 ✅ Earned value: $108k of $180k. (margin status: not tracked)
example.note: One ledger, no margin view, no thresholds — close-out review will discover the margin hole when nothing can be done about it.
